what: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
text: R package downloading data Office National Statistics.

title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
text: Office National Statistics UK’s national statistical authority. produces headline economic data drives policy markets - GDP, inflation, unemployment, wages, trade, house prices, retail sales, population estimates. releases move sterling, set agenda Bank England’s Monetary Policy Committee, underpin economic research across UK. ONS publishes thousands time series website, identified four-character CDID code. data freely available, using programmatically requires knowing codes, constructing download URLs topic paths dataset identifiers, parsing CSV files metadata headers, handling mixed-frequency data annual, quarterly, monthly observations appear single file. package replaces named functions return clean data frames.

what: How is this different from existing packages?
title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
text: onsr package CRAN wraps ONS Beta API - API host headline economic time series. GDP, CPI, unemployment, wages, trade data available . onsr useful Census data regional breakdowns, access series people actually want. last actively developed 2021. package takes different approach. fetches data directly ONS website’s CSV time series endpoint, covers every published CDID. get named functions - ons_gdp(), ons_cpi(), ons_unemployment() - handle series codes, URL construction, frequency filtering, caching internally.

title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
text: data freely available, using programmatically requires knowing obscure CDID codes (GDP growth IHYQ, CPI annual rate D7G7), constructing URLs topic paths dataset identifiers, parsing CSV files mix metadata headers data rows, filtering duplicate observations annual, quarterly, monthly data appear file. Every analyst works ONS data writes boilerplate.
code: # Without this package url <- paste0(   "https://www.ons.gov.uk/generator?format=csv",   "&uri=/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/timeseries/ihyq/pn2" ) raw <- readLines(url) # ... skip metadata rows, parse periods, filter frequency, handle errors ...  # With this package library(ons) ons_gdp(from = "2020-01-01")

title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
code: install.packages("ons")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/ons")

code: # Average weekly earnings (GBP) wages <- ons_wages(from = "2024-01-01") tail(wages, 6) #>         date value #>   2025-07-01   728 #>   2025-08-01   733 #>   2025-09-01   734 #>   2025-10-01   740 #>   2025-11-01   740 #>   2025-12-01   735  # Year-on-year growth or real terms ons_wages(measure = "growth", from = "2024-01-01") ons_wages(measure = "real", from = "2024-01-01")

code: # Trade balance (GBP millions, negative = deficit) trade <- ons_trade(from = "2024-01-01") tail(trade, 6) #>         date value #>   2025-07-01 -2152 #>   2025-08-01 -2151 #>   2025-09-01 -4530 #>   2025-10-01 -5790 #>   2025-11-01 -5564 #>   2025-12-01 -4340  # Exports or imports separately ons_trade(measure = "exports", from = "2024-01-01")

what: Fetching any series by CDID code
title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
code: # If you know the ONS CDID code, use ons_get() directly # Find codes at https://www.ons.gov.uk/timeseriestool  # GDP growth vs CPI inflation ons_get(c("IHYQ", "D7G7"), from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-06-30") #>         date cdid value #>   2024-01-01 IHYQ   0.8 #>   2024-04-01 IHYQ   0.6 #>   2024-01-01 D7G7   4.0 #>   2024-02-01 D7G7   3.4 #>   2024-03-01 D7G7   3.2 #>   2024-04-01 D7G7   2.3 #>   2024-05-01 D7G7   2.0 #>   2024-06-01 D7G7   2.0  # Search for series by keyword ons_search("GDP growth")

title: Download Data from the Office for National Statistics
text: downloads cached locally user cache directory. Subsequent calls return cached copy instantly - network request made.
code: # Force a fresh download ons_gdp(from = "2020-01-01", cache = FALSE)  # Remove files older than 7 days clear_cache(max_age_days = 7)  # Remove all cached files clear_cache()

what: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
title: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
text: Removes cached data files stored package. default cached files deleted. Use max_age_days remove files older given number days.

title: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
code: clear_cache(max_age_days = NULL)

title: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
text: max_age_days Numeric. supplied, files older many days removed. NULL (default), cached files removed.

title: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect deleting cached files.

title: Clear the local download cache — clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ons.cache_dir = tempdir()) clear_cache() #> Warning: cannot remove file '/tmp/RtmpAfic1p/bslib-246362e7e3ff6191071d5f9b40ba8d62', reason 'Directory not empty' #> Warning: cannot remove file '/tmp/RtmpAfic1p/downlit', reason 'Directory not empty' #> ✔ Removed 2 cached files. options(op) # }

what: ons: Download Data from the 'Office for National Statistics' — ons-package
title: ons: Download Data from the 'Office for National Statistics' — ons-package
text: Provides functions download tidy statistical data published 'Office National Statistics' https://www.ons.gov.uk. Covers GDP, inflation (CPI, CPIH, RPI), unemployment, employment, wages, trade, retail sales, house prices, productivity, population, public sector finances. series fetched 'ONS' website using CSV time series endpoint. House price data sourced 'HM Land Registry' https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/land-registry. Data cached locally sessions.

title: ons: Download Data from the 'Office for National Statistics' — ons-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

title: Download consumer price inflation — ons_cpi
text: Downloads CPI, CPIH, RPI data ONS. Returns either annual rate change index level.

title: Download consumer price inflation — ons_cpi
code: ons_cpi(   measure = c("cpi", "cpih", "rpi"),   type = c("rate", "index"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Download consumer price inflation — ons_cpi
text: https://www.ons.gov.uk

title: Download consumer price inflation — ons_cpi
text: measure Character. One "cpi" (default), "cpih", "rpi". type Character. One "rate" (default, annual percentage change) "index" (index level). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults NULL (available data). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults NULL. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download consumer price inflation — ons_cpi
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day month. value Numeric. Inflation rate (percent) index level.

code: ons_employment(   group = c("total", "male", "female"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Download employment rate — ons_employment
text: https://www.ons.gov.uk

title: Download employment rate — ons_employment
text: group Character. One "total" (default), "male", "female". Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults NULL (available data). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults NULL. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download employment rate — ons_employment
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day period. value Numeric. Employment rate (percent).

title: Download GDP data — ons_gdp
text: Downloads quarterly Gross Domestic Product data ONS. Available 1948 (levels) 1955 (growth rates).

title: Download GDP data — ons_gdp
code: ons_gdp(   measure = c("growth", "yoy", "level", "nominal"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   cache = TRUE )

title: Download GDP data — ons_gdp
text: https://www.ons.gov.uk

title: Download GDP data — ons_gdp
text: measure Character. One "growth" (default, quarter--quarter percentage change), "yoy" (year--year percentage change), "level" (chained volume measure GBP millions, seasonally adjusted), "nominal" (current price GBP millions, seasonally adjusted). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date. Defaults NULL (available data). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date. Defaults NULL. cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download GDP data — ons_gdp
text: data frame columns: date Date. First day quarter. value Numeric. GDP value (percent growth measures, GBP millions level measures).

what: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
text: Fetches time series ONS website using CDID (four-character series identifier). function uses ONS Search API discover download path, retrieves CSV.

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
code: ons_get(cdid, from = NULL, to = NULL, cache = TRUE)

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
text: https://www.ons.gov.uk

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
text: cdid Character vector. One ONS CDID codes (e.g. "IHYQ" quarterly GDP growth). Maximum 50 codes per call. Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). Start date filter. Defaults NULL (available data). Date character (YYYY-MM-DD). End date filter. Defaults NULL (latest observation). cache Logical. Use cached data available (default TRUE).

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
text: data frame columns: date Date. Observation date (first day period). cdid Character. CDID code. value Numeric. Observation value.

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get
text: CDID codes can found browsing ONS time series explorer https://www.ons.gov.uk/timeseriestool using ons_search().

title: Download any ONS time series by CDID code — ons_get

what: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
text: Searches ONS catalogue time series matching keyword phrase. Returns CDID codes, titles, dataset information can used ons_get().

title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
code: ons_search(query, limit = 10L)

title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
text: https://www.ons.gov.uk

title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
text: query Character. Search term (e.g. "GDP growth", "unemployment"). limit Integer. Maximum number results return (default 10, maximum 50).

title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
text: data frame columns: cdid Character. four-character CDID code. dataset_id Character. ONS dataset identifier. title Character. Description series. release_date Character. Date recent release.

title: Search for ONS time series — ons_search
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ons.cache_dir = tempdir()) try(ons_search("GDP growth")) #>    cdid dataset_id                                         title #> 1  KB82        PN2                 Revisions to GDP CVM growth % #> 2  KB82        QNA                 Revisions to GDP CVM growth % #> 3  IHYT        QNA GDP Deflator: Quarter on quarter growth: SA % #> 4  IHYS       UKEA       GDP Deflator: Year on Year growth: SA % #> 5  IHYS        QNA       GDP Deflator: Year on Year growth: SA % #> 6  MNF4        PN2 GDP Deflator: Quarter on quarter growth: SA % #> 7  MNF3        QNA       GDP Deflator: Year on Year growth: SA % #> 8  MNF4        QNA GDP Deflator: Quarter on quarter growth: SA % #> 9  IHYT        PN2 GDP Deflator: Quarter on quarter growth: SA % #> 10 IHYT       UKEA GDP Deflator: Quarter on quarter growth: SA % #>                release_date #> 1  2026-02-12T00:00:00.000Z #> 2  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 3  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 4  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 5  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 6  2026-02-12T00:00:00.000Z #> 7  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 8  2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z #> 9  2026-02-12T00:00:00.000Z #> 10 2026-03-30T23:00:00.000Z options(op) # }

title: ons 0.1.4
text: Added rate limiting ons_search() match API functions prevent accidental 429 errors.

title: ons 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 Examples now wrapped try() handle transient ONS API failures gracefully CRAN checks.

title: ons 0.1.2
text: Removed non-existent pkgdown URL DESCRIPTION.

title: ons 0.1.1
text: Examples now cache tempdir() instead user’s home directory, fixing CRAN policy compliance \donttest examples. Cache directory now configurable via options(ons.cache_dir = ...).

title: ons 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-12 Initial release. Data functions: ons_gdp(), ons_monthly_gdp(), ons_cpi(), ons_unemployment(), ons_employment(), ons_inactivity(), ons_wages(), ons_trade(), ons_retail_sales(), ons_house_prices(), ons_productivity(), ons_population(), ons_public_finances(). Generic fetch: ons_get() fetches series CDID code. Discovery: ons_search() searches ONS catalogue keyword. Caching: clear_cache() manages locally cached data.
